query: Find this citation in Page Index and tell me which exact filename matches it.
why: Primary goal is Page Index resolution to a verified file.

query: I have this messy bibliography entry — can you resolve it to the correct Page Index paper?
why: Bibliography-like input that needs conservative Page Index matching.

query: Check whether this Page Index filename is real and still points to the intended paper.
why: Direct filename verification is in scope for the finder.

query: Find these three papers in Page Index first; I need the verified filenames before doing anything else.
why: Explicit upstream resolution task before later workflows.

query: Read this verified Page Index file all the way through and tell me whether every page was covered.
Summarize this paper from Page Index into a Markdown memo.
why: Page Index summarization belongs to pageindex-summarize-papers.

query: Classify this Page Index paper into my taxonomy.
why: Classification is downstream of finding and not owned here.

query: Download this paper from the web if it is missing from Page Index.
why: External retrieval is outside the Page Index-only resolution boundary.
